name: TAO Failed Break Repair v1
edge_mechanism: failed break repair back into balance with front-loaded harvesting
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing failed-break repair in a broad enough form to avoid the already-killed strict failed-breakdown-hold grammar. The edge should come from rejection of failed directional acceptance and quick repair back into value.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-break repair remains too sparse, if repairs lack enough travel after reacceptance, or if the higher-volatility lane turns the setup into unstructured noise.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks away from balance, fails to secure acceptance, reclaims value, and travels enough in the repair direction for partial harvesting before noise returns.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a 100% chop regime, failed breaks should occur more often than clean persistent breaks. A broad repair expression should participate more honestly than the previously killed strict hold grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is appropriate as a structurally different stress-test lane because failed breaks and sharp repairs are more visible there.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If failed breaks only snap back to value with no travel beyond the first touch, the repair thesis will not survive costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If repair entries trade with density but continuation is shallow, front-load exits further rather than adding more confirmation layers.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A decent number of short-hold repair trades with first-target harvesting doing most of the expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus at least one cleaner lane show real repair density and enough post-cost travel after reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or repairs that die immediately at value.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: controlled by broad failed-break reclaim logic instead of exact candle sequencing

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive stress-test lane for failed-break repair behavior.
validation_basket.continue_if: Repairs show real density and enough post-cost travel beyond the reclaim.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The setup becomes too noisy or only touches value without extension.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Visible failed breaks followed by sharp value repairs and short-hold continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same repair mechanism generalizes to a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the repair shape with cleaner execution.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only exists on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Smaller but cleaner failed-break repairs than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether failed-break repair is structural across a second major asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows comparable density and repair travel after reclaim.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism vanishes on majors and survives only on TAO.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Less volatile but still tradable failed-break repair structure.
